title: Programmable Decentralized Feature Extraction Telemetry
author: Ismail, L.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: As networks grow in scale and diversity, real-time network monitoring becomes increasingly vital and must align with the dynamic requirements of AI-driven analysis. Fixed-feature telemetry lacks the adaptability required to support this diversity, and aggregated monitoring data is insufficient, as critical anomalies may appear at the level of individual flows. This paper proposes a novel Decentralized Feature Extraction Telemetry (DFET) solution for enhancing the flexibility of P4 programmable switches by enabling the control plane to selectively extract only the relevant features of packets belonging to specific flows, according to the monitoring and application requirements. The introduced technique has been described and evaluated in terms of latency, CPU utilization, and system scalability.
